manifest.name: View Mode Rules

name: View Mode Rules

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**View Mode Rules 1.0.0 — initial release**

Set a default view (editing or reading) per note or folder, without touching frontmatter.

### Highlights
- Right-click any note or folder in the file explorer to set its default view.
- Folder rules apply to every note inside the folder; the deepest matching folder wins, and a note-level rule overrides any folder rule.
- Plugin-level global default (Editing, Reading, or "Use Obsidian default") as a fallback — Obsidian's built-in behavior applies beneath that.
- Rules survive renames and moves (including descendants of a renamed folder). Rules for deleted notes are cleaned up automatically.
- Optional: re-apply configured views when navigating into an already-open tab (on by default).
- Optional: apply configured views to tabs restored at Obsidian startup (off by default, to avoid overriding the user's manual toggle).
- Commands to set or clear the current note's rule, plus an "Open settings" command.

### Install
- **Manual:** download `manifest.json` and `main.js` from this release and copy them into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/view-mode-rules/`, then enable under `Settings → Community plugins`.
- **Community plugins browser:** pending approval in the Obsidian community directory.

### Scope
Only Markdown notes are affected. Non-Markdown file types (PDFs, canvases, images, etc.) are ignored.

### Compatibility
Requires Obsidian 1.5.0 or later. Works on desktop and mobile.
